Patch Set 1:

Why return 0 for some cases, -EROFS in others?

I'm not saying that is wrong, but some explanation in a comment or the commit message would be useful. 

Also, is this something we can add a test for?

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

> Why return 0 for some cases, -EROFS in others?

There are two main cases for the modifications against the read only device:

1. Client sponsored modifications. For these kinds of modifications against read only device, the server will return -EROFS. That will make related client/application to know the failure. But the server can run normally.

2. Servers internally sponsored modifications. Most of these modification may happen during the system mount. If we return -EROFS to related caller, it may cause mount failure. Because it is not easy to strictly distinguish who sponsors the modification, then as long as it may affect the server mount or system running, then we will drop related modifications silently.

For this case, the llog related modifications may be related with Lustre configuration or DNE update logs. So "RETURN(0)" can avoid failing the mount or recovery. If we can have more hints for the modification sponsors, we can distinguish them more detailed. But now, skip the modification silently may be the safe way.
Patch Set 1: -Code-Review

Patch Set 1:

llog_create and llog_write definitely need to return ENOFS so the caller can run through it's error path.
llog_cancel_rec says 0 is success and 1 is success & log destroyed, so 0 seems reasonable in this case.
Maybe llog_destroy should return ENOFS...
Uploaded patch set 2.

Patch Set 1:

If the system is still mountable and non-blocked, then returning -EROFS is the first choice; otherwise, if not sure, drop the modification silently.

Patch Set 3: Code-Review+2

Patch Set 3:

> Onyx Autotest started enforced custom session. The following test requirements were specified: trivial=true testlist=sanity-lsnapshot mdtcount=4 mdscount=2 ostfilesystemtype=zfs mdtfilesystemtype=zfs

I do NOT think above test case can always trigger llog_destroy() during mdd_changelog_llog_init(); otherwise we should have hit LU-11193 when landing the lsnapshot patches a long time ago. But in fact, we never hit it before. So it should be very rare case for the conditional calling llog_destroy() during mdd_changelog_llog_init().

What I worry about is that returning -EROFS for some llog modification(s) during the mount of the snapshot (as readonly) may cause the mdd_prepare() failure as to the snapshot cannot be mounted. I would suggest to add some fail_loc to guarantee that the llog modification can be triggered during the lsnapshot tests, then verify whether the system is accessible or not with your patch applied.

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

Mark -1 per Nasf comment.

Patch Set 3: Code-Review+1

I'm changing my review back to +1 because I'd like to get this fix landed.  It isn't a regression vs. the current code/tests, and fixes a known issue that is otherwise causing real problems for users.

Nathaniel, can you please file a separate ticket linked to LU-11193 to track additional testing for this patch.

Patch Set 3:

In response to Nasf.  We only hit this for DNE with ZFS (which I don't think was enabled as a test when snapshot code landed).  It can hit occasionally without DNE, but seems much less likely (experimentally).

Patch Set 3: Code-Review+1

> In response to Nasf.  We only hit this for DNE with ZFS (which I don't think was enabled as a test when snapshot code landed).  It can hit occasionally without DNE, but seems much less likely (experimentally).

Yes, on some degree, it can explain why we did not hit such trouble before. I will mark this patch as "+1" to make things to move forward.

But what I really worry about is that your patch may resolve one issue but may introduce new trouble as my former comment. So please make more tests (may be another separated patch) with fail_loc stub to simulate the llog modification during mount snapshot with your patch to avoid some bad cases.
Patch Set 3: Code-Review+2
Change has been successfully cherry-picked as 12a695d5b2fea73ee5edb7a0a79d557c94cafa35 by Oleg Drokin

commit.subject: LU-11193 llog: Do not write to read-only devices
commit.message:
LU-11193 llog: Do not write to read-only devices

Check if device is read-only before trying to start a transaction
on a devices.  Lustre snapshots are generally read-only.

When underlying device is read-only we check in the following places:
* llog_destroy, llog_write - return EROFS
* llog_open_create - on the create sized we return EROFS
* llog_cancel_rec - we return 0 which means success where 1 means
"success & log destroyed"

Test-Parameters: trivial testlist=sanity-lsnapshot mdtcount=4 mdscount=2 ostfilesystemtype=zfs mdtfilesystemtype=zfs
Signed-off-by: Nathaniel Clark <nclark@whamcloud.com>
